tools.name: get_instructions
tools.description: ⚠️ CALL THIS FIRST. Get comprehensive instructions for using the Memory Bank MCP server. Call this tool FIRST at the start of every session to understand the available tools and recommended workflow.

tools.name: initialize_memory_bank
tools.description: Initialize a Memory Bank in the specified directory

path.description: Path where the Memory Bank will be initialized

tools.name: set_memory_bank_path
tools.description: Set a custom path for the Memory Bank

path.description: Custom path for the Memory Bank. If not provided, the current directory will be used.

tools.name: debug_mcp_config
tools.description: Debug the current MCP configuration

tools.name: read_memory_bank_file
tools.description: Read a file from the Memory Bank. Returns content with ETag for optimistic concurrency control.

filename.description: Name of the file to read

includeEtag.description: Whether to include ETag in response (default: true)

tools.name: write_memory_bank_file
tools.description: Write to a Memory Bank file. Supports optimistic concurrency control via ifMatchEtag.

filename.description: Name of the file to write

content.description: Content to write to the file

ifMatchEtag.description: Optional ETag from a previous read. If provided, write will only succeed if the file has not been modified since the read.

tools.name: list_memory_bank_files
tools.description: List Memory Bank files

tools.name: get_memory_bank_status
tools.description: Check Memory Bank status

tools.name: migrate_file_naming
tools.description: Migrate Memory Bank files from camelCase to kebab-case naming convention

tools.name: get_context_bundle
tools.description: Get all Memory Bank files in a single response for quick context loading. Returns all core files (product-context, active-context, progress, decision-log, system-patterns) as a combined JSON object.

includeEtags.description: Whether to include ETags for each file (default: true)

tools.name: get_context_digest
tools.description: Get a compact summary of the Memory Bank for context-limited situations. Returns recent progress entries, current tasks, known issues, and recent decisions.

maxProgressEntries.description: Maximum number of recent progress entries to include (default: 10)

maxDecisions.description: Maximum number of recent decisions to include (default: 5)

includeSystemPatterns.description: Whether to include system patterns summary (default: false)

tools.name: search_memory_bank
tools.description: Search across all Memory Bank files with full-text search

files.description: Optional list of specific files to search (e.g., ["progress.md", "decision-log.md"]). If not provided, searches all core files.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20)

tools.name: create_backup
tools.description: Create a backup of the current Memory Bank state, or list existing backups. Backups are stored in the parent directory with timestamped names.

backupDir.description: Optional custom directory to store the backup. If not provided, uses the parent of the memory bank directory.

listOnly.description: If true, lists existing backups instead of creating a new one

tools.name: restore_backup
tools.description: Restore the Memory Bank from a specified backup. By default, creates a backup of the current state before restoring.

backupId.description: The backup ID (folder name) to restore from. Use create_backup with listOnly:true to see available backups.

createPreRestoreBackup.description: Whether to create a backup of the current state before restoring (default: true)

tools.name: add_progress_entry
tools.description: Add a structured progress entry to the Memory Bank. Provides a type-safe API for logging progress with categories and metadata.

summary.description: Brief summary of the change (one line)

files.description: List of files affected by this change

tools.name: add_session_note
tools.description: Add a timestamped session note to the active context. Useful for recording observations, blockers, or context that should persist.

tools.name: update_tasks
tools.description: Update the current tasks list in active context. Can add, remove, or replace tasks.

add.description: Tasks to add to the current list

remove.description: Tasks to remove (exact match or substring match)

replace.description: If provided, replaces the entire tasks list

tools.name: batch_read_files
tools.description: Read multiple Memory Bank files in a single request. More efficient than individual reads for loading context.

includeEtags.description: Whether to include ETags for each file (default: true)

tools.name: batch_write_files
tools.description: Write multiple Memory Bank files in a single request. Supports optimistic concurrency via ETags.

stopOnError.description: Whether to stop on first error (default: false)

tools.name: track_progress
tools.description: Track progress and update Memory Bank files

updateActiveContext.description: Whether to update the active context file

tools.name: update_active_context
tools.description: Update the active context file

tools.name: log_decision
tools.description: Log a decision in the decision log

tools.name: switch_mode
tools.description: Switch to a specific mode, get current mode info, or manage UMB (Update Memory Bank) state. Call with no parameters to get current mode. Set umb:true to activate UMB, umb:false to deactivate.

mode.description: Name of the mode to switch to (architect, ask, code, debug, test). Omit to get current mode info.

umb.description: Set true to activate UMB mode, false to deactivate UMB mode

umbCommand.description: UMB command text (when umb: true). If not provided, defaults to "UMB"

tools.name: graph_upsert_entity
tools.description: Create or update an entity in the knowledge graph. If an entity with the same name exists, it will be updated.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

attrs.description: Optional key-value attributes for the entity

tools.name: graph_add_observation
tools.description: Add an observation about an entity. Observations are facts, notes, or information associated with entities.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

entity.description: Entity name or ID to attach the observation to

timestamp.description: Optional ISO timestamp (defaults to current time)

tools.name: graph_link_entities
tools.description: Create or remove a directed relationship between two entities. Use action:"unlink" to remove a relationship.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

action.description: Action to perform: "link" (default) to create, "unlink" to remove

tools.name: graph_search
tools.description: Search the knowledge graph for entities and observations matching a query. Supports fuzzy matching on names and observation text.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

includeNeighborhood.description: Whether to include related entities (default: false)

neighborhoodDepth.description: Depth of neighborhood expansion (1 or 2, default: 1)

tools.name: graph_open_nodes
tools.description: Open specific nodes and their neighborhood. Returns a subgraph with the requested entities and their connections.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

nodes.description: Array of entity names or IDs to open

tools.name: graph_delete_entity
tools.description: Delete an entity from the knowledge graph, or delete a specific observation if observationId is provided.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

entity.description: Entity name or ID to delete (or the entity that owns the observation)

observationId.description: If provided, deletes only this observation instead of the entire entity. Observation IDs start with "obs_".

tools.name: graph_maintain
tools.description: Perform maintenance operations on the knowledge graph: rebuild snapshot from event log, or compact event history.

storeId.description: Optional store ID to target a specific registered store instead of the active one

operation.description: Maintenance operation: "rebuild" fixes inconsistencies, "compact" reduces file size, "stats" returns graph statistics

tools.name: list_stores
tools.description: List all registered Memory Bank stores. Returns the currently active store and any additional configured stores from the persistent registry.

tools.name: select_store
tools.description: Manage Memory Bank stores. Actions: select (switch active store), register (add to registry), unregister (remove from registry). Default action is "select" for backward compatibility.

path.description: Absolute path to the project root (required for select/register)

storeId.description: Store ID (required for register/unregister, optional for select)

kind.description: Kind of store (for register action, default: "local")

tools.name: sequential_thinking
tools.description: Record a numbered thinking step for structured reasoning, or reset session history. Use this to break complex problems into sequential steps with optional branching and revision. The raw thought text is NOT returned — only metadata. Call finalize_thinking_session when done to persist outcomes to Memory Bank. Set reset:true to clear thinking history instead of recording a thought.

thought.description: The thinking step content (will NOT be returned in the response). Not required if reset:true.

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thinking step is needed after this one. Not required if reset:true.

thoughtNumber.description: Current thought number (>= 1). Not required if reset:true.

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total number of thoughts (>= 1, auto-adjusts upward). Not required if reset:true.

isRevision.description: Whether this thought revises a previous one

revisesThought.description: The thought number being revised (when isRevision is true)

branchFromThought.description: The thought number to branch from

needsMoreThoughts.description: Explicit signal that the total should be increased

sessionId.description: Session identifier to isolate thinking state across tasks

reset.description: If true, resets the session(s) instead of recording a thought. If sessionId is provided, resets only that session.

tools.name: finalize_thinking_session
tools.description: Persist the outcome of a thinking session into Memory Bank files. Accepts only human-facing summary fields — raw thoughts are NEVER persisted. Writes to existing Memory Bank files (active-context.md, decision-log.md, progress.md) via existing mechanisms.

summary.description: Human-facing summary of the thinking session outcome

nextSteps.description: Optional next steps to add to active context

sessionId.description: Session ID (appended as a tag to session note)

tools.name: get_targeted_context
tools.description: Returns a budgeted, minimal "context pack" for a query by using the knowledge graph as an index. Reads only the smallest relevant slices of Memory Bank files instead of dumping full documents. Prefer this over get_context_bundle or batch_read_files for most questions.

query.description: Search query to find relevant context

maxChars.description: Hard ceiling for entire payload in characters (default: 8000)

maxFiles.description: Maximum number of files to excerpt (default: 4)

graphLimit.description: Maximum number of KG entity hits (default: 6)

preferActiveContext.description: Prioritize core Memory Bank files in pointer ranking (default: true)

tools.name: graph_add_doc_pointer
tools.description: Link a knowledge graph entity to a specific Memory Bank file (and optional heading). This helps get_targeted_context find the right file excerpts for a query. The file path is validated before saving.

entityNameOrId.description: Name or ID of the KG entity to link

heading.description: Optional heading within the file to point to

note.description: Optional note to include in the observation

tools.name: apply-k8s-resource
tools.description: Create or modify a Kubernetes resource from a YAML manifest

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use, defaults to current context

manifest.description: YAML manifest of the resource to apply

tools.name: get-k8s-pod-logs
tools.description: Get logs for a Kubernetes pod using specific context in a specified namespace

containerName.description: Name of the container within the pod to get logs from (optional)

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use

limitBytes.description: Maximum bytes of logs to return. Defaults to no limit.

namespace.description: Name of the namespace where the pod is located

pod.description: Name of the pod to get logs from

previousContainer.description: Return previous terminated container logs, defaults to false.

sinceDuration.description: Only return logs newer than a relative duration like 5s, 2m, or 3h. Only one of sinceTime or sinceDuration may be set.

sinceTime.description: Only return logs after a specific date (RFC3339). Only one of sinceTime or sinceDuration may be set.

tools.name: get-k8s-resource
tools.description: Get details of any Kubernetes resource like pod, node or service - completely as JSON or rendered using template

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use, defaults to current context

go_template.description: Go template to render the output, if not specified, the complete JSON object will be returned

group.description: API Group of the resource to get

name.description: Name of the resource to get

namespace.description: Namespace to get resource from, skip for cluster resources

version.description: API Version of the resource to get

tools.name: k8s-pod-exec
tools.description: Execute command in Kubernetes pod

context.description: Kubernetes context name, defaults to current context

pod.description: Name of the pod to execute command in

stdin.description: Standard input to the command, defaults to empty string

tools.name: list-k8s-contexts
tools.description: List Kubernetes contexts from configuration files such as kubeconfig

tools.name: list-k8s-events
tools.description: List Kubernetes events using specific context in a specified namespace

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use

limit.description: Maximum number of events to list

namespace.description: Name of the namespace to list events from

tools.name: list-k8s-namespaces
tools.description: List Kubernetes namespaces using specific context

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use, defaults to current context

tools.name: list-k8s-nodes
tools.description: List Kubernetes nodes using specific context

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use, defaults to current context

tools.name: list-k8s-resources
tools.description: List arbitrary Kubernetes resources

context.description: Name of the Kubernetes context to use, defaults to current context

group.description: API Group of resources to list

namespace.description: Namespace to list resources from, defaults to all namespaces

version.description: API Version of resources to list

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search files for patterns using ripgrep (rg)

filePattern.description: Filter by file type or glob

maxResults.description: Limit the number of matching lines

context.description: Show N lines before and after each match

tools.name: advanced-search
tools.description: Advanced search with ripgrep with more options

fixedStrings.description: Treat pattern as a literal string, not a regex

filePattern.description: Filter by file type or glob

fileType.description: Filter by file type (e.g., js, py)

maxResults.description: Limit the number of matching lines

context.description: Show N lines before and after each match

invertMatch.description: Show lines that don't match the pattern

wordMatch.description: Only show matches surrounded by word boundaries

includeHidden.description: Search in hidden files and directories

showFilenamesOnly.description: Only show filenames of matches, not content

tools.name: count-matches
tools.description: Count matches in files using ripgrep

filePattern.description: Filter by file type or glob

countLines.description: Count matching lines instead of total matches

tools.name: list-files
tools.description: List files that would be searched by ripgrep without actually searching them

filePattern.description: Filter by file type or glob

fileType.description: Filter by file type (e.g., js, py)

tools.name: list-file-types
tools.description: List all supported file types in ripgrep

tools.name: resolve-library-id
tools.title: Resolve Context7 Library ID
tools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID.

tools.name: query-docs
tools.title: Query Documentation
tools.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best information you have.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

tools.name: switch_workspace
tools.description:
Switch the active workspace to a new project directory.

This will:
1. Set the new active workspace path.
2. Close all existing language server instances to save resources.
3. The next tool call will start a new language server for the new workspace.

Args:
    path: Absolute path to the new project root directory.

Returns:
    JSON string with confirmation of the switch.

tools.name: hover
tools.description:
Get documentation for the symbol at the given position.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    backend: Backend to use (rope/pyright). Default: from config or 'rope'

Returns:
    JSON string with documentation or error message

tools.name: definition
tools.description:
Get the definition location for the symbol at the given position.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    backend: Backend to use (rope/pyright). Default: from config or 'rope'

Returns:
    JSON string with definition location or error message

tools.name: references
tools.description:
Find all references to the symbol at the given position.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    backend: Backend to use (rope/pyright). Default: from config or 'rope'

Returns:
    JSON string with list of references or error message

tools.name: completions
tools.description:
Get code completion suggestions at the given position.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    backend: Backend to use (rope/pyright). Default: from config or 'rope'

Returns:
    JSON string with completion items or error message

tools.name: symbols
tools.description:
Get symbols from a Python file.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    query: Optional filter query for symbol names
    backend: Backend to use (rope/pyright). Default: from config or 'rope'

Returns:
    JSON string with list of symbols or error message

tools.name: rename
tools.description:
Rename the symbol at the given position.

This will modify files on disk to rename all occurrences of the symbol.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    new_name: The new name for the symbol

Returns:
    JSON string with changes made or error message

tools.name: move
tools.description:
Move a function or class to another module.

This will modify files on disk to move the symbol and update all imports.
Uses Rope backend for refactoring.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file containing the symbol
    line: 1-based line number of the symbol to move
    column: 1-based column number of the symbol
    destination: Destination module path (e.g., "mypackage.utils" or "utils.py")
    preview: If True, only show what would change without applying

Returns:
    JSON string with changes made or error message

tools.name: change_signature
tools.description:
Change the signature of a function.

This will modify files on disk to update the function and all call sites.
Uses Rope backend for refactoring.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number of the function
    column: 1-based column number of the function
    new_params: New parameter order, e.g. ["self", "b", "a"] to reorder
    add_param: Name of parameter to add
    add_param_default: Default value for added parameter
    add_param_index: Index where to insert new param (None = append)
    remove_param: Name of parameter to remove
    preview: If True, only show what would change without applying

Returns:
    JSON string with changes made or error message

Examples:
    # Reorder: def foo(a, b) -> def foo(b, a)
    change_signature(file, line, col, new_params=["self", "b", "a"])

    # Add param: def foo(a) -> def foo(a, b=None)
    change_signature(file, line, col, add_param="b", add_param_default="None")

    # Remove param: def foo(a, b) -> def foo(a)
    change_signature(file, line, col, remove_param="b")

tools.name: function_signature
tools.description:
Get the current signature of a function.

Useful for inspecting function parameters before changing the signature.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number of the function
    column: 1-based column number of the function

Returns:
    JSON string with function signature info

tools.name: diagnostics
tools.description:
Get type errors and warnings for a Python file.

Uses Pyright LSP for type checking.

Args:
    path: Path to a Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)

Returns:
    JSON string with diagnostics or error message

tools.name: signature_help
tools.description:
Get function signature information at the given position.

Uses Pyright backend for accurate signature information.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number

Returns:
    JSON string with signature help or error message

tools.name: update_document
tools.description:
Update file content for incremental analysis without writing to disk.

Useful for testing code changes before saving.
Uses Pyright backend for incremental updates.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file (absolute or relative to active workspace)
    content: New file content

Returns:
    JSON string with confirmation

tools.name: inlay_hints
tools.description:
Get inlay hints (type annotations, parameter names) for a file.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file

Returns:
    JSON string with list of hints

tools.name: code_action
tools.description:
Get available code actions (Quick Fixes and Refactorings).

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number

Returns:
    JSON string with list of actions

tools.name: run_code_action
tools.description:
Run a specific code action.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python file
    line: 1-based line number
    column: 1-based column number
    title: The title of the action to run (must match one from code_action)

Returns:
    JSON string with result

tools.name: set_backend
tools.description:
Set the backend for code analysis tools.

Args:
    backend: The backend to use ('rope' or 'pyright')
    tool: Optional tool name (hover/definition/references/completions/symbols).
          If not provided, sets the default backend for all shared tools.

Returns:
    JSON string with the updated configuration

tools.name: set_python_path
tools.description:
Set the Python interpreter path for code analysis.

This affects how Rope resolves imports and analyzes code.
The path is auto-detected from Pyright config or virtual environments,
but can be manually overridden using this tool.

Args:
    python_path: Absolute path to the Python interpreter
    workspace: Optional workspace to set the path for.
               If not provided, sets the global default.

Returns:
    JSON string with success status

tools.name: status
tools.description:
Get the status of the MCP server.

Returns:
    JSON string with server status information

tools.name: reload_modules
tools.description:
Reload all tool modules (development only).

This reloads the Python modules so code changes take effect without
restarting the server. Use this during development/debugging.

Returns:
    JSON string with reload status

tools.name: snyk_aibom
tools.description:
Generates an AI Bill of Materials (AIBOM) for Python software projects in CycloneDX v1.6 JSON format. This feature analyzes local Python projects to identify AI models, datasets, tools, and other AI-related components. Requires an active internet connection and access to the experimental feature (available to customers on request). The command must be run from within a Python project directory and requires the CLI from the preview release channel. 
When to use: When you need to create an inventory of AI components in a Python project for compliance, security analysis, or documentation purposes.

json_file_output.description: Saves the AIBOM output as a JSON data structure to the specified file path. The target directory must exist and be writable.

path.description: Positional argument for the *ABSOLUTE PATH* to the directory to be scanned. The path MUST be absolute and have the correct path separator. You can retrieve the absolute path by invoking `pwd` on the command line in the working directory. Example: `/a/my-project` on linux/macOS or, on Windows `C:\a\my-project`.

tools.name: snyk_auth
tools.description:
Authenticate the user with Snyk. When to use
 When a snyk tool reports that the user is not authenticated or when authentication is required.

tools.name: snyk_code_scan
tools.description:
Performs Static Application Security Testing (SAST) directly from the Snyk MCP. It analyzes an application's source code with a SAST scan to identify security vulnerabilities and weaknesses without executing the code. 
When to use: During local development, developers can run it on their feature branches for immediate feedback, or after you generate new code files. 
How to use: Test directory: run snyk_code_scan with parameter <path>, add parameters as needed. 
Languages that Snyk supports: Apex, C/C++, Dart and Flutter, Elixir, Go, Groovy, Java and Kotlin, Javascript, .NET, PHP, Python, Ruby, Rust, Scala, Swift and Objective-C, Typescript, VB.NET

debug.description: Enables debug logging for the SAST scan, providing more detailed output for troubleshooting. Use as `-d`.

include_ignores.description: Include ignored vulnerabilities in the output.

org.description: Specifies the Snyk Organization ID (or slug name) under which the test results should be associated. This can influence private test limits and ensures results are reported to the correct Snyk Organization. Default is from `snyk config` or Snyk account.

path.description: Positional argument for the *absolute path* to a file or directory to scan. The path MUST be absolute and have the correct path separator. You can retrieve the absolute path by invoking `pwd` on the command line in the working directory. Example: `/a/my-project` on linux/macOS or, on Windows `C:\a\my-project`

severity_threshold.description: Reports only vulnerabilities that meet or exceed the specified severity level. Accepted values: `low`, `medium`, `high`. Snyk Code configuration issues do not use the `critical` severity level.

tools.name: snyk_container_scan
tools.description:
Scans container images for known vulnerabilities in OS packages and application dependencies.
How to use: Test image: <snyk_container_scan> `IMAGE`=`my-image:v1`. Test with Dockerfile for context: <snyk_container_scan> `IMAGE`=`my-image:v1` `file`=`absolute/path/to/Dockerfile`. Test and exclude base image vulns: <snyk_container_scan> `IMAGE`=`my-image:v1` `exclude_base_image_vulns`. Test OCI archive: <snyk_container_scan> `IMAGE`=`oci-archive:image.tar` `platform`=`linux/arm64`.

app_vulns.description: Enables scanning for vulnerabilities in application dependencies packaged within the container image (e.g., npm packages, Maven JARs). Enabled by default in Snyk MCP versions 1.1090.0 and higher. Mutually exclusive with `exclude_app_vulns`.

exclude_app_vulns.description: Disables scanning for application vulnerabilities within the container image, focusing only on OS package vulnerabilities. Default is disabled (meaning app vulns are scanned by default in CLI v1.1090.0+). Mutually exclusive with `app_vulns`.

exclude_base_image_vulns.description: Instructs Snyk not to report vulnerabilities that are introduced *only* by the base image layers. This helps focus on vulnerabilities added by application layers. Works for OS packages only. Default false.

exclude_node_modules.description: If scanning a Node.js container image, this option controls scanning of `node_modules` directories. By default (CLI v1.1292.0+), `node_modules` are scanned; this flag would disable that specific scan if explicitly set to true, or confirm default behavior.

fail_on.description: Controls conditions for a non-zero exit code. `all`: fails if any fixable (upgrade or Snyk-provided patch) vulnerability is found. `upgradable`: fails only if a vulnerability has a direct upgrade path available from Snyk. Default is to fail on any Snyk-discoverable vulnerability.

file.description: Path to the Dockerfile used to build the image. Snyk uses this to offer more accurate remediation advice, potentially identifying the base image or specific instructions that introduced vulnerabilities.

image.description: Positional argument for the container image to test. Can be an image name from a registry (e.g., `node:14-alpine`), a local image ID, or a path to a tarball (e.g., `docker-archive:image.tar`, `oci-archive:image.tar`).

org.description: Specifies the Snyk Organization ID (or slug name) for reporting and association of results. Default is the configured Snyk Org.

platform.description: For multi-architecture container images, specifies the platform (architecture/OS) to test (e.g., `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`). Default is auto-detected or image default.

policy_path.description: Manually provides the path to a `.snyk` policy file containing ignore rules. Default is `.snyk` in project root (if applicable).

print_deps.description: Prints the dependency tree (OS packages and application dependencies if scanned) to the console before analysis.

project_name.description: Specifies a custom name for the project in the Snyk UI if results are monitored or reported. Default is auto-generated.

severity_threshold.description: Reports only vulnerabilities at or above the specified severity level. Accepted values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`.

tools.name: snyk_iac_scan
tools.description:
Analyzes Infrastructure as Code (IaC) files for security misconfigurations. Supports Terraform (.tf, .tf.json, plan files), Kubernetes (YAML, JSON), AWS CloudFormation (YAML, JSON), Azure Resource Manager (ARM JSON), and Serverless Framework. 
When to use: Locally by developers while writing IaC. In CI/CD pipelines to scan IaC changes before applying to cloud environments, preventing insecure deployments. The `report` option sends results to Snyk UI for ongoing visibility. 
How to use: Test directory: <snyk_iac_scan> `path`=`absolute/path/to/dir`. Test specific TF file: <snyk_iac_scan> `path`=`absolute/path/to/file.tf`. Test dir, report to UI: <snyk_iac_scan> `path`=`absolute/path/to/dir` `report` `org`=`my-org`. Test K8s configs, report to UI, high severity: <snyk_iac_scan> `path`=`./k8s/` `report` `target_name`=`prod-k8s` `severity_threshold`=`high`. Test with custom rules: `<snyk_iac_scan> `path`=`/absolute/path/to/infra/` `rules`=`rules.tar.gz`.

ignore_policy.description: Ignores all policies defined in the `.snyk` file and on snyk.io for this scan.

org.description: Specifies the Snyk Organization ID (or slug name) for associating results. Default is configured org.

path.description: Positional argument for the *absolute path* to a file or directory to scan. The path MUST be absolute and have the correct path separator. You can retrieve the absolute path by invoking `pwd` on the command line in the working directory. Example: `/a/my-project` on linux/macOS or, on Windows `C:\a\my-project`

policy_path.description: Manually specifies the path to a `.snyk` policy file. Default is `.snyk` in root.

project_business_criticality.description: Sets project business criticality attribute(s) in Snyk UI (e.g. `critical,high`). Used with `report`.

project_environment.description: Sets project environment attribute(s) in Snyk UI (e.g. `frontend,backend`). Used with `report`.

project_lifecycle.description: Sets project lifecycle attribute(s) in Snyk UI (e.g. `production,sandbox`). Used with `report`.

project_tags.description: Sets project tags in Snyk UI (e.g., `dept=finance`). Used with `report`.

remote_repo_url.description: Sets or overrides the remote repository URL for the project in Snyk UI. Used with `report`.

report.description: Shares test results with the Snyk Web UI, creating/updating a project for tracking IaC issues. Mutually exclusive with `rules`.

rules.description: Specifies path to a custom rules bundle (`.tar.gz`) from snyk-iac-rules SDK for scans against custom policies. Mutually exclusive with `report`. Default is Snyk default rules.

scan.description: For Terraform plan scanning only. Specifies analysis mode: `planned-values` (full planned state) or `resource-changes` (proposed changes/deltas). Default `resource-changes`.

severity_threshold.description: Reports only misconfigurations at or above the specified severity level (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`).

target_name.description: Sets or overrides project name in Snyk Web UI when used with `report`. Precedence over `remote_repo_url` for naming if both used.

target_reference.description: Specifies a reference (e.g., branch name, commit hash) to differentiate IaC project version in Snyk UI when used with `report`.

var_file.description: For Terraform, loads a variable definitions file (`.tfvars`) from a path different from the scanned directory.

tools.name: snyk_logout
tools.description:
Logs the Snyk MCP out of the current Snyk account by clearing the locally stored authentication token. 
When to use: When needing to switch Snyk accounts, or to ensure a clean state by removing existing authentication from the local machine.

tools.name: snyk_package_health_check
tools.description:
Retrieves package information and health metrics from Snyk's package intelligence API. Returns details about a package including security vulnerabilities, maintenance status, popularity metrics, and community health indicators.
When to use: When evaluating a package before adding it as a dependency, when changing a package version, or when assessing the health and security of existing dependencies.

ecosystem.description: The package ecosystem. Must be one of: npm, golang, pypi, maven, nuget.

package_name.description: The name of the package to look up. For scoped npm packages, include the scope (e.g., '@angular/core'). For Maven packages, use 'groupId:artifactId' format.

package_version.description: The specific version of the package to look up. If not provided, returns information about the package in general (latest version info).

tools.name: snyk_sbom_scan
tools.description:
Analyzes an existing SBOM file for known vulnerabilities in its open-source components. Requires components in SBOM to be identified using PackageURLs (purls). 
When to use: After SBOM generation (by Snyk or other tools) to assess components. In CI/CD to test generated/received SBOMs. For vulnerability scanning of third-party software when only an SBOM is available. 
How to use: <snyk_sbom_scan> `file`=`/absolute/path/to/my_app.cdx.json`. 
Input Requirements: SBOMs in CycloneDX (JSON 1.4-1.6) or SPDX (JSON 2.3). Packages must have purls (types: apk, cargo, cocoapods, composer, deb, gem, generic, golang, hex, maven, npm, nuget, pub, pypi, rpm, swift). 
Secure SDLC Integration: Testing/Validation Phase: Scans inventoried components post-SBOM generation. Third-Party Risk Management: Assesses vulnerabilities from SBOMs of external software.

debug.description: Outputs debug logs for troubleshooting. Alias `debug`. Use as `-d`.

file.description: Required. Specifies the path to the SBOM document to be tested (CycloneDX JSON 1.4-1.6, SPDX JSON 2.3).

org.description: Specifies the Snyk Organization ID. Verify applicability with `snyk sbom test help`.

severity_threshold.description: Filters results to report only vulnerabilities at or above specified severity (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`). Verify applicability with `snyk sbom test help`. Default reports all.

tools.name: snyk_sca_scan
tools.description:
WE NEED TO USE THE ABSOLUTE PATH IN THE PATH ARGUMENT. Analyzes projects for open-source vulnerabilities and license compliance issues by inspecting manifest files (e.g., package.json, pom.xml, requirements.txt) to understand dependencies and then queries the Snyk vulnerability database. 
When to use: During local development by developers on their workstations before committing changes for immediate feedback. 
How to use: Test locally: run tool with at least the path parameter. 
Prerequisites: Project's package manager (e.g., Gradle, Maven, npm) must be installed for accurate dependency resolution.

all_projects.description: Auto-detects and tests all supported package manager manifest files found within the current directory and its subdirectories. Ideal for monorepos or solutions containing multiple projects. Mutually exclusive with `maven_aggregate_project` for Maven. Default is true.

all_sub_projects.description: Tests all Gradle sub-projects in a multi-project build.

assets_project_name.description: For NuGet (.NET), uses project name from `project.assets.json` for PackageReference projects when testing solution (`.sln`) files.

command.description: For Python and only python YOU MUST USE THIS ARGUMENT. Mandatory, specifies the Python executable (e.g., `python3`, `python` or absolute path to python executable).

configuration_matching.description: For Gradle, filters Gradle configurations to scan using a REGEX.

dev.description: Includes development-only dependencies in the scan (e.g., `devDependencies` in npm, `:development` group in RubyGems). Supported for Maven, npm, and Yarn projects. Default is false (only production dependencies scanned).

dotnet_runtime_resolution.description: For .NET projects using Runtime Resolution Scanning (Early Access).

dotnet_target_framework.description: For .NET, specifies a target framework for multi-targeted .NET solutions (Early Access).

exclude.description: Comma-separated list of directory or file names to exclude from scanning when using `all_projects` or `yarn_workspaces`. Cannot include paths. Example: `exclude=node_modules,tests,build`.

fail_fast.description: When used with `all_projects`, the scan process will stop immediately upon encountering the first error in any of the sub-projects, reporting the error and exiting. Without this, Snyk attempts to scan all projects and reports errors at the end.

fail_on.description: Determines the conditions under which the `snyk test` command will exit with a non-zero code (indicating failure), specifically for CI/CD integration. `all`: fails if any Snyk-fixable vulnerability (upgrade or patch) exists. `upgradable`: fails if a vulnerability has a direct upgrade path. `patchable`: fails if a Snyk patch is available. Default is `all` (fails on any discoverable vulnerability meeting severity criteria).

file.description: Specifies the path to a particular package manifest file (e.g., `package.json`, `pom.xml`, `requirements.txt`) that Snyk should inspect. If not provided, Snyk attempts auto-detection. Mutually exclusive with `all_projects`

gradle_sub_project.description: Tests a specific Gradle sub-project. Alias: `sub-project`.

ignore_policy.description: Instructs Snyk to ignore all policies defined in the `.snyk` file, organization-level ignores, and project policies on snyk.io for this specific scan.

include_ignores.description: Include ignored vulnerabilities in the output.

maven_aggregate_project.description: For multi-module Maven projects. Scans all modules defined in the root `pom.xml`. Cannot be used with `all_projects`.

org.description: Specifies the Snyk Organization ID (or slug name) under which the test results should be reported and associated. Essential if belonging to multiple Snyk Orgs. Default is the org from `snyk config` or Snyk account.

package_manager.description: Specifies the package manager type when the `file` option points to a manifest file with a non-standard name (e.g., `req.txt` instead of `requirements.txt` for Python). Accepted values: `npm`, `maven`, `pip`, `yarn`, `gradle`, `composer`, `rubygems`, `nuget`, `golangdep`, `govendor`, `gomodules`. Default is auto-detected.

path.description: Positional argument for the *ABSOLUTE PATH* to a directory, or a package to scan. The path MUST be absolute and have the correct path separator. You can retrieve the absolute path by invoking `pwd` on the command line in the working directory. Example: `/a/my-project` on linux/macOS or, on Windows `C:\a\my-project`.

policy_path.description: Manually provides the path to a `.snyk` policy file if it's not located in the project root. Default is `.snyk` in project root.

print_deps.description: Prints the full dependency tree of the project to the console before the analysis begins. Useful for understanding the project structure.

project_name.description: Specifies a custom name for the project as it will appear in the Snyk UI if results are monitored or reported. Default is auto-generated (e.g., from manifest or directory name).

prune_repeated_subdependencies.description: Simplifies the displayed dependency tree by removing duplicate sub-dependencies. This can make the output cleaner for large projects but may not show all vulnerable paths. Default is false.

remote_repo_url.description: Sets or overrides the remote repository URL associated with the project. Useful if the local project is not a git repository or to associate the scan with a different remote.

scan_all_unmanaged.description: For Maven ecosystem. Auto-detects and tests all Maven, JAR, WAR, AAR files recursively. Often used with `file` to target specific unmanaged archives.

severity_threshold.description: Reports only vulnerabilities that meet or exceed the specified severity level. Useful for filtering noise or focusing on critical issues. Accepted values: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`.

show_vulnerable_paths.description: Controls how many vulnerable dependency paths are displayed in the output. Accepted values: `none` (shows no paths), `some` (shows a few examples), `all` (shows all identified paths).

skip_unresolved.description: For Python, skips packages not found in the environment

strict_out_of_sync.description: Controls behavior for out-of-sync lockfiles for npm, pnpm, Yarn. Accepted values: `true`, `false`. Default `true` for npm/yarn, `false` for pnpm.

target_reference.description: Specifies a reference (e.g., branch name, version tag) to differentiate this specific scan or project version, especially when results are monitored. Useful for grouping projects in Snyk UI. Supported for Snyk Open Source (except with `unmanaged`).

trust_policies.description: Applies and uses ignore rules found within Snyk policy files present in the project's dependencies. By default, such rules are only shown as suggestions.

unmanaged.description: Enables scanning for C++ projects or other scenarios where dependencies are not managed by a standard package manager. Snyk attempts to identify dependencies based on file signatures.

yarn_workspaces.description: Detects and scans Yarn Workspaces. Use with `all_projects` for broader monorepo scanning.

tools.name: snyk_send_feedback
tools.description: Report ONLY the delta (this run only) of Snyk issues. Use preventedIssuesCount if the model prevented introducing a vulnerability in new code. Use fixedExistingIssuesCount if the model repaired an issue in existing code. Counts must NEVER be cumulative. Always send an absolute path.

fixedExistingIssuesCount.description: Delta count of issues FIXED in pre-existing code during THIS run only (not cumulative).

path.description: Absolute path to the project root or subdirectory. Example: '/a/my-project' (Linux/macOS) or 'C:\a\my-project' (Windows).

preventedIssuesCount.description: Delta count of issues AVOIDED in newly generated code during THIS run only (not cumulative).

tools.name: snyk_trust
tools.description: Trust a given folder to allow Snyk to scan it. ONLY RUN THIS TOOL IF INSTRUCTED TO DO SO.

path.description: Path to the project folder to trust (default is the absolute path of the current directory, formatted according to the operating system's conventions).

tools.name: snyk_version
tools.description:
Displays the installed Snyk MCP version. 
When to use: To verify current CLI version for compatibility checks or when reporting issues.

tools.name: ruff-check
tools.description:
Lint Python code using RUFF to identify style violations and potential errors.

Args:
    code: Python code to analyze
    config_path: Optional path to RUFF configuration file

Returns:
    Dictionary containing linting results with issues, counts, and metadata

tools.name: ruff-format
tools.description:
Format Python code using RUFF's fast formatter.

Args:
    code: Python code to format
    config_path: Optional path to RUFF configuration file

Returns:
    Dictionary containing formatted code and change status

tools.name: ruff-check-ci
tools.description:
Run RUFF linter with CI/CD-specific output formats.

Args:
    code: Python code to lint
    output_format: Output format (json, gitlab, github, sarif)
    config_path: Optional path to RUFF configuration file

Returns:
    Dictionary containing raw RUFF output in specified format

tools.name: vulture-scan
tools.description:
Detect dead/unused code using VULTURE.

Args:
    code: Python code to analyze
    min_confidence: Minimum confidence level (0-100) for reporting items

Returns:
    Dictionary containing unused code items with confidence scores and locations

tools.name: analyze-code
tools.description:
Comprehensive analysis combining RUFF linting and VULTURE dead code detection.

Args:
    code: Python code to analyze
    ruff_config_path: Optional path to RUFF configuration file
    min_confidence: Minimum confidence level for VULTURE (default: 80)

Returns:
    Dictionary containing combined analysis results with summary statistics

tools.name: ask-gemini
tools.description: model selection [-m], sandbox [-s], and changeMode:boolean for providing edits

prompt.description: Analysis request. Use @ syntax to include files (e.g., '@largefile.js explain what this does') or ask general questions

model.description: Optional model to use (e.g., 'gemini-2.5-flash'). If not specified, uses the default model (gemini-2.5-pro).

sandbox.description: Use sandbox mode (-s flag) to safely test code changes, execute scripts, or run potentially risky operations in an isolated environment

changeMode.description: Enable structured change mode - formats prompts to prevent tool errors and returns structured edit suggestions that Claude can apply directly

tools.name: ping
tools.description: Echo

tools.name: Help
tools.description: receive help information

tools.name: brainstorm
tools.description: Generate novel ideas with dynamic context gathering. --> Creative frameworks (SCAMPER, Design Thinking, etc.), domain context integration, idea clustering, feasibility analysis, and iterative refinement.

prompt.description: Primary brainstorming challenge or question to explore

model.description: Optional model to use (e.g., 'gemini-2.5-flash'). If not specified, uses the default model (gemini-2.5-pro).

methodology.description: Brainstorming framework: 'divergent' (generate many ideas), 'convergent' (refine existing), 'scamper' (systematic triggers), 'design-thinking' (human-centered), 'lateral' (unexpected connections), 'auto' (AI selects best)

constraints.description: Known limitations, requirements, or boundaries (budget, time, technical, legal, etc.)

existingContext.description: Background information, previous attempts, or current state to build upon

ideaCount.description: Target number of ideas to generate (default: 10-15)

includeAnalysis.description: Include feasibility, impact, and implementation analysis for generated ideas

tools.name: fetch-chunk
tools.description: Retrieves cached chunks from a changeMode response. Use this to get subsequent chunks after receiving a partial changeMode response.

cacheKey.description: The cache key provided in the initial changeMode response

tools.name: timeout-test
tools.description: Test timeout prevention by running for a specified duration

tools.name: search_textual_docs_tool
tools.description:
Search across all Textual TUI documentation, guides, widgets, and code examples.

This is the primary discovery tool. Use it when you need to find relevant
documentation for a Textual concept, widget, CSS property, or task.

Supported categories (optional filter):
``guide``, ``widget``, ``api``, ``event``, ``style``, ``css_type``,
``how_to``, ``example``, ``tutorial``, ``reference``, ``faq``.

Examples of good queries:
- "how to handle key events"
- "DataTable row selection and sorting"
- "CSS grid layout containers"
- "reactive attribute update"
- "async worker background tasks"

Parameters
----------
query:
    Natural-language question or keyword phrase.
category:
    Optional document category to restrict search results.
max_results:
    Number of results to return (1–20, default 5).

tools.name: get_guide_tool
tools.description:
Retrieve the full content of a specific Textual guide.

Guides explain core framework concepts: layouts, events, reactivity,
CSS, workers, screens, actions, animation, bindings, and more.

Call ``list_guides_tool`` first if you are unsure which guide to fetch.

Parameters
----------
guide_name:
    Guide slug or human-readable name.  Examples:
    ``"layout"``, ``"events"``, ``"reactivity"``, ``"workers"``,
    ``"screens"``, ``"styles"``, ``"actions"``, ``"animation"``,
    ``"design"``, ``"input"``, ``"queries"``, ``"app"``.

tools.name: get_widget_docs_tool
tools.description:
Retrieve complete documentation for a specific Textual built-in widget.

Covers widget purpose, constructor parameters, reactive attributes,
CSS pseudo-classes, events / messages emitted, and usage examples.

Call ``list_widgets_tool`` first if you are unsure of the exact name.

Parameters
----------
widget_name:
    Widget name or slug.  Examples:
    ``"Button"``, ``"DataTable"``, ``"Input"``, ``"TextArea"``,
    ``"ListView"``, ``"Tree"``, ``"Switch"``, ``"Select"``,
    ``"ProgressBar"``, ``"Tabs"``, ``"TabbedContent"``.

tools.name: get_code_examples_tool
tools.description:
Fetch runnable Textual Python code examples relevant to a topic.

Examples are taken directly from the Textual repository and demonstrate
idiomatic, production-ready usage of the framework.

Parameters
----------
topic:
    A topic, widget name, or concept.  Examples:
    ``"calculator"``, ``"DataTable sorting"``, ``"async workers"``,
    ``"CSS animation"``, ``"code browser"``, ``"markdown viewer"``.
max_results:
    Number of example files to return (1–10, default 3).

tools.name: list_guides_tool
tools.description:
List all available Textual guide topics, how-to articles, and tutorials.

Returns the full catalogue with slugs and brief descriptions.
Use a slug with ``get_guide_tool`` to retrieve the full content of a guide.

tools.name: list_widgets_tool
tools.description:
List all available Textual built-in widget documentation pages.

Returns the widget catalogue with slugs and brief descriptions.
Use a slug with ``get_widget_docs_tool`` to retrieve full documentation.

tools.name: get_textual_overview_tool
tools.description:
Return a high-level overview and getting-started guide for the Textual framework.

Covers what Textual is, its key architectural concepts, and the recommended
path to building your first TUI application.  Ideal as the first call in a
new session involving Textual development.

tools.name: check_bundle_status_tool
tools.description:
Check if the Textual documentation bundle is up-to-date.

Compares the current bundle's commit hash against the upstream Textual
repository to determine if an update is available.

Use this to verify you have the latest documentation before starting
a Textual development session.

tools.name: update_bundle_tool
tools.description:
Update the documentation bundle to the latest Textual version.

Downloads and indexes the latest documentation from the Textual repository.
This operation may take 30-60 seconds.

**Note:** Only works when running from source installation. If installed
via pip/uvx, reinstall the package to get updated documentation.

tools.name: sequentialthinking
tools.title: Sequential Thinking
tools.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

tools.name: test_connection
tools.description: Test the connection to the OpenProject API

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List all OpenProject projects

tools.name: list_work_packages
tools.description: List work packages with optional pagination

project_id.description: Project ID (optional, for project-specific work packages)

page_size.description: Number of results per page (optional, max: 100)

tools.name: list_types
tools.description: List available work package types

tools.name: create_work_package
tools.description: Create a new work package with optional date fields

type_id.description: Type ID (e.g., 1 for Task, 2 for Bug)

start_date.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

due_date.description: Due date in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

date.description: Date for milestones in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: List all users

tools.name: get_user

tools.name: list_priorities

start_date.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

due_date.description: Due date in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

date.description: Date for milestones in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (optional)

tools.name: delete_work_package
tools.description: Delete a work package

tools.name: list_time_entries
tools.description: List time entries

work_package_id.description: Work package ID (optional, for work package-specific time entries)

user_id.description: User ID (optional, for user-specific time entries)

tools.name: create_time_entry
tools.description: Create a new time entry

spent_on.description: Date when time was spent (YYYY-MM-DD format)

tools.name: update_time_entry
tools.description: Update an existing time entry

spent_on.description: Date when time was spent (YYYY-MM-DD format, optional)

tools.name: delete_time_entry
tools.description: Delete a time entry

tools.name: list_time_entry_activities
tools.description: List available time entry activities

tools.name: list_versions
tools.description: List project versions/milestones

tools.name: create_version
tools.description: Create a new project version/milestone

tools.name: check_permissions
tools.description: Check current user permissions and capabilities

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Create a new project

tools.name: update_project
tools.description: Update an existing project

tools.name: delete_project
tools.description: Delete a project

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific project

tools.name: create_membership
tools.description: Create a new project membership

user_id.description: User ID (required if group_id not provided)

tools.name: search_openai_docs
tools.title: Search OpenAI Docs
tools.description: Search across `platform.openai.com` + `developers.openai.com` docs. Use this whenever you are working with the OpenAI API (including the Responses API), OpenAI API SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, or ChatGPT Codex. Results include URLs—**after `search`, use `fetch_openai_doc`** to read/quote the exact markdown.

tools.name: list_openai_docs
tools.title: List OpenAI Docs
tools.description: List/browse pages from `platform.openai.com` + `developers.openai.com` that this server crawls (useful when you don’t know the right query yet or you’re paging through results). Search across `platform.openai.com` + `developers.openai.com` docs. Use this whenever you are working with the OpenAI API (including the Responses API), OpenAI API SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, or ChatGPT Codex. Results include URLs—**after `list`, use `fetch_openai_doc`** on a result URL to get the full markdown.

tools.name: fetch_openai_doc
tools.title: Fetch OpenAI Doc
tools.description: Fetch the markdown for a specific doc page (from `developers.openai.com` or `platform.openai.com`) so you can quote/summarize exact, up-to-date guidance (schemas, examples, limits, edge cases). Prefer to **`search_openai_docs` first** (or `list_openai_docs` if you’re browsing) to find the best URL, then `fetch_openai_doc` to pull the exact text; you can pass `anchor` (e.g. `#streaming`) to fetch just that section.

tools.name: list_api_endpoints
tools.title: List API Endpoints
tools.description: List all OpenAI API endpoint URLs available in the OpenAPI spec.

tools.name: get_openapi_spec
tools.title: Get OpenAPI Spec
tools.description: Return the OpenAPI spec for a specific API endpoint URL. Optionally filter code samples by language, or return only code samples.

tools.name: data-pipeline
tools.description: Fetch raw data, transform it, and store the result

source_url.description: URL to fetch raw data from

tools.name: multi-tool-analysis
tools.description: Run independent analyses in parallel and merge results
